title: Acid-Fast Bacillus (AFB) Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
the difference between an inactive and active TB infection. So, if the screening test comes back positive, your provider may want to verify the results by using an AFB test. AFB tests may also be used to help diagnose other types of AFB infections. These include:
Leprosy, a once feared, but rare and easily treatable disease caused by Mycobacterium leprae and Mycobacterium lipomatosis that affects the nerves, eyes, and skin. The skin often becomes red and flaky, with a loss of feeling. 
An infection similar to TB, such as Mycobacterium avium , that mostly affects people with HIV and others with weakened immune systems . 
AFB tests may also be used for people who have already been diagnosed with TB. The tests can show if the treatment is working and whether the infection can still be spread to others.
Why do I need an AFB test? 
You may need an AFB test if you have symptoms of active TB (TB disease). Which symptoms you have will depend on where in the body the TB bacteria are growing.
If TB disease is in the lungs (pulmonary), symptoms may include:
Cough that lasts for three weeks or more 
Coughing up blood and/or sputum 
Chest pain 
Other symptoms of TB disease may include:
Chills and fever 
Fatigue 
Night sweats (heavy sweating during sleep) 
Losing weight without trying 
Back pain and paralysis (for spinal TB) 
Blood in your urine 
Headache and coma (for TB meningitis )
